Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – Call participants cannot hear the user
Ticket number: TI-1616128
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 07
Owner: Employee 33
Origin: Telephone
Type: Incident
Created on: 14.10.25 09:18
Closure
Closure date: 14.10.25 09:52
Closure text: Microphone not properly connected. Device configured correctly. Function restored.
Activities
14.10.25 09:10 – Telephone report
Mr. Contact Person 07 reports that an employee can hear everything during Teams calls but cannot be heard by the other participant.
Both internal and external calls are affected.
Chat works.
Camera works.
14.10.25 09:18
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 33.
14.10.25 09:25 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good morning
Could you please briefly confirm:
– Is a headset or the integrated microphone being used?
– Does the issue also occur during a Teams test call?
Thank you.
14.10.25 09:32 – Customer response
A USB headset is being used.
No sound is recorded during the test call either.
14.10.25 09:40 – Remote session
Connected with the user.

However, the input level indicator remains empty.
In the Windows Sound menu, no input signal is displayed either.

After correctly plugging it in, the input level indicator immediately showed activity.
Test call performed → Microphone functioning.
14.10.25 09:50 – User feedback
Call tested. Working properly again.
14.10.25 09:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 07
The microphone was not properly connected, which prevented any input signal from being detected.
After correctly reconnecting the device, voice transmission is functioning normally again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Token caching / Conditional Access evaluation delay and CA policy adjustment: TI-1571046
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 38:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF25: SharePoint – Access works only partially after permission change
Ticket number: TI-1571046
Company: Customer Company 25 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 05
Owner: Employee 21
Origin: Portal
Type: Incident
Created on: 04.03.25 13:18
Closure
Closure date: 11.03.25 16:02
Closure text: Root cause identified (Token caching & Conditional Access evaluation delay). Access stable after policy adjustment.
Activities
04.03.25 13:18
Ticket created via customer portal
Description:
Several employees report that after being added to the project “ERP-Upgrade”, they generally have access to SharePoint, but the folder
/Projects/ERP_Upgrade/Finance
is not displayed.
In some cases, access works for certain users after logging out and back in, but not for others.
Error message:
Access denied or item not found
04.03.25 13:32
Ticket assigned to: Employee 21
Status: In Progress
Notes:
04.03.25 14:05 – Initial analysis
• Affected users are members of Azure AD group “ERP_Finance”
• This group is a member of “SP_ERP_Project”
• SharePoint permissions correctly configured
• Comparison with working user shows identical group structure
Test:
Direct access via URL → partially successful
Notable:
Users with activated MFA requirement report issues more frequently.
04.03.25 15:40 – Conditional Access review
Tenant uses Conditional Access Policy:
“Require compliant device for Finance Resources”
Policy was adjusted on 28.02.
Some users access from private devices.
Device status in Entra ID partially “unknown”.
05.03.25 09:10 – Token analysis
Azure Sign-In Logs reviewed:
• Multiple entries with “Conditional Access not satisfied”
• At the same time, successful token issuance for SharePoint Root
Hypothesis:
Token was issued before group change → contains outdated claims
Users who had not fully logged out were working with cached tokens.
05.03.25 10:25 – Test measure
• User fully logged out (Office + browser)
• Browser cache cleared
• Device compliance status checked
Result:
Access possible afterward.
06.03.25 11:50 – In-depth analysis
Issue reoccurs for 2 users.
Difference identified:
These users access via Hybrid Azure AD Joined Devices.
Device synchronization was faulty.
Device compliance status was only updated after 6–8 hours.
07.03.25 09:30 – Actions
• Conditional Access Policy adjusted:
→ SharePoint project folder no longer included in Finance-specific CA rule
• Users re-authenticated
• Device sync triggered manually
10.03.25 – Monitoring
No further access issues reported.
Sign-In Logs unremarkable.
11.03.25 16:02 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 05
The root cause was not group nesting, but a delay in Conditional Access policy evaluation combined with cached authentication tokens.
After policy adjustment and renewed authentication, access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: This is a brief issue/title describing token caching and Conditional Access evaluation delay with a policy adjustment reference, not providing any recommendation or directive.
groundedness_reason: The ticket TI-1571046 closure and activity notes state the root cause was token caching and Conditional Access evaluation delay, and that access became stable after the Conditional Access policy was adjusted.

SOURCE 42:

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – Camera not detected
Ticket number: TI-1615042
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 09
Owner: Employee 33
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 07.10.25 09:12
Closure
Closure date: 07.10.25 11:06
Closure text: Camera available again in Teams. Root cause was missing operating system permission and blocked device setting. Steps performed according to Blueprint.
Activities
07.10.25 09:05 – Email from Contact Person 09
Good morning
For one user, the camera is no longer working in Teams. No camera is displayed in the Teams device settings. In other apps it also appears to be partially unavailable. Could you please assist?
Kind regards
07.10.25 09:12
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 33.
07.10.25 09:30 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Could you please confirm whether the user is using Windows or Mac and whether the camera is available in the browser (e.g., Teams Web)?
Thank you.
07.10.25 09:48 – Customer response
Windows 11. The camera also does not work in the browser.
07.10.25 10:25 – Internal note
Connected to the user via remote session. The camera is visible in Device Manager, but Teams has no access. In Windows privacy settings, camera access for apps is disabled. Additionally, Teams is not enabled in the list of allowed apps.
According to “Blueprint – Teams Client Devices & Permissions – Version 1.2”, the standard checks were performed (OS privacy settings, app permissions, Teams restart).
07.10.25 10:55 – Customer feedback
User confirms that the camera is now displayed in Teams and the test call is successful.
07.10.25 11:06 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 09
The camera was blocked by operating system permissions. After adjusting the camera and app permissions according to the Blueprint, the camera is available again in Teams.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: This is reporting a cause/diagnosis (camera missing due to OS/privacy permissions blocking Teams) rather than offering a recommendation or proposed action.
groundedness_reason: Ticket TI-1615042 explicitly states Windows privacy settings had camera access for apps disabled and Teams was not allowed; closure notes the root cause was missing OS permission/blocked device setting and adjusting permissions restored the camera.

→ Access remains denied

09.04.25 11:40

For two affected users in Entra ID portal, attribute
“source” = Windows Server AD
For functioning external users:
“source” = Azure AD

09.04.25 14:20
Ticket escalated for in-depth analysis.
Assigned Group changed to: BU Infrastructure – Identity
10.04.25 08:55
Ticket taken over by: System Engineer 14
In-depth analysis
10.04.25 09:40
Azure AD Connect reviewed.
Observation:
Group “COMPLIANCE_TEAM” exists both On-Prem and in Cloud.
On-Prem version is synchronized.
Cloud version was originally created manually.
Result:
• External guests exist only in Cloud group
• SharePoint references the synchronized On-Prem group
Same name, but different Object IDs.
10.04.25 11:25
Review of group members via Graph:
Cloud group:
→ Contains external guests
Synchronized On-Prem group:
→ Contains only internal users
Since SharePoint evaluates by Object ID, not by name, external users lose access once synchronization takes effect.
11.04.25 08:30
Reproduction in test:
• On-Prem group re-synchronized
• Cloud group overwritten
• External users reproducibly lose access
Root Cause
Two groups existed with identical name:
COMPLIANCE_TEAM
1. Cloud-native group
2. Synchronized On-Prem group
Through Azure AD Connect, the Cloud group was gradually overwritten or deactivated.
SharePoint referenced the synchronized version.
External guests were never managed On-Prem and therefore were not included in the synchronized group.
Actions
11.04.25 – 14.04.25
• Consolidation of group structure
• Old Cloud group removed
• On-Prem group properly cleaned
• External guests managed as Cloud-only members
• Azure AD Connect full synchronization performed
15.04.25 – Monitoring
Access remains stable
No further inconsistencies
17.04.25 15:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 07
The root cause was the parallel existence of two groups with identical names but different origins (Cloud vs. On-Prem synchronized).
Due to synchronization, effective group membership for external users was not correctly applied.
The group structure was consolidated and fully synchronized.
Access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

COMPLIANCE_TEAM

Through Azure AD Connect, the Cloud group was gradually overwritten or deactivated.
SharePoint referenced the synchronized version.
External guests were never managed On-Prem and therefore were not included in the synchronized group.
Actions
11.04.25 – 14.04.25

• Azure AD Connect full synchronization performed

Dear Contact Person 07

The group structure was consolidated and fully synchronized.
Access is now stable.

Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: What the records document: external users can lose effective access when two groups with identical names exist (one Cloud, one synchronized On‑Prem). SharePoint evaluates group membership by objectId; synchronized On‑Prem groups can overwrite cloud groups and drop external guests that were only in the cloud group. Consolidation and full Azure AD Connect sync fixed that scenario and restored external access in the documented incident

10.04.25 08:55
Ticket taken over by: System Engineer 14
In-depth analysis
10.04.25 09:40
Azure AD Connect reviewed.
Observation:
Group “COMPLIANCE_TEAM” exists both On-Prem and in Cloud.
On-Prem version is synchronized.
Cloud version was originally created manually.
Result:
• External guests exist only in Cloud group
• SharePoint references the synchronized On-Prem group
Same name, but different Object IDs.
10.04.25 11:25
Review of group members via Graph:
Cloud group:
→ Contains external guests
Synchronized On-Prem group:
→ Contains only internal users
Since SharePoint evaluates by Object ID, not by name, external users lose access once synchronization takes effect.
11.04.25 08:30
Reproduction in test:
• On-Prem group re-synchronized
• Cloud group overwritten
• External users reproducibly lose access
Root Cause
Two groups existed with identical name:
COMPLIANCE_TEAM
1. Cloud-native group
2. Synchronized On-Prem group
Through Azure AD Connect, the Cloud group was gradually overwritten or deactivated.
SharePoint referenced the synchronized version.
External guests were never managed On-Prem and therefore were not included in the synchronized group.
Actions
11.04.25 – 14.04.25
• Consolidation of group structure
• Old Cloud group removed
• On-Prem group properly cleaned
• External guests managed as Cloud-only members
• Azure AD Connect full synchronization performed
15.04.25 – Monitoring
Access remains stable
No further inconsistencies
17.04.25 15:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 07
The root cause was the parallel existence of two groups with identical names but different origins (Cloud vs. On-Prem synchronized).
Due to synchronization, effective group membership for external users was not correctly applied.
The group structure was consolidated and fully synchronized.
Access is now stable.
Kind regards

Azure AD Connect reviewed.
Observation:

10.04.25 11:25
Review of group members via Graph:
Cloud group:
→ Contains external guests
Synchronized On-Prem group:
→ Contains only internal users
Since SharePoint evaluates by Object ID, not by name, external users lose access once synchronization takes effect.
11.04.25 08:30
Reproduction in test:
• On-Prem group re-synchronized
• Cloud group overwritten
• External users reproducibly lose access
Root Cause
Two groups existed with identical name:
COMPLIANCE_TEAM
1. Cloud-native group
2. Synchronized On-Prem group
Through Azure AD Connect, the Cloud group was gradually overwritten or deactivated.
SharePoint referenced the synchronized version.
External guests were never managed On-Prem and therefore were not included in the synchronized group.
Actions
11.04.25 – 14.04.25
• Consolidation of group structure
• Old Cloud group removed
• On-Prem group properly cleaned
• External guests managed as Cloud-only members
• Azure AD Connect full synchronization performed
15.04.25 – Monitoring
Access remains stable
No further inconsistencies
17.04.25 15:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 07
The root cause was the parallel existence of two groups with identical names but different origins (Cloud vs. On-Prem synchronized).
Due to synchronization, effective group membership for external users was not correctly applied.
The group structure was consolidated and fully synchronized.
Access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
